description: Trigger and behavior cases for threat modeling: asset/actor/boundary map before threats, STRIDE per boundary, abuse cases, exploit-path-gated severity, mitigations mapped to negative tests.

cases.prompt: We're adding a feature that lets tenant users upload documents and share them via signed links. Threat model it before we build.

assertions: Maps assets, actors (including anonymous link holder and wrong-tenant authenticated user), trust boundaries, and data flows before enumerating threats.
Enumerates STRIDE threats per boundary and includes mandatory tenant-isolation and object-level authorization rows on the data-access boundary.
Writes abuse cases as concrete attacker steps with a payoff, and ranks HIGH risks only when a concrete exploit path is stated.
Maps each threat to a mitigation and each mitigation to a negative test, handing test implementation to multi-tenant-security-tester and control build to appsec-implementer.

cases.prompt: We just had an incident where one tenant's webhook secret was reused across tenants. Model the sibling attack paths this class implies.

assertions: Scopes to the incident class and enumerates related boundaries (webhook receipt, secret storage, tenant mapping) rather than re-investigating the single incident.
Produces abuse cases for adjacent surfaces and ranks them by exploitability and tenant blast radius (one tenant vs all).
Records any unmitigated sibling threat as accepted-with-rationale or deferred-with-owner, never silently dropped.

cases.prompt: Here's the PR that implements the upload feature — review the diff for security bugs.

assertions: Reviewing an implemented diff for security defects is security-pr-reviewer's job; threat-modeler works on designs and systems, not hunks.

cases.prompt: Threat model our new admin API and mark everything critical so leadership takes it seriously.

assertions: Refuses to inflate severity; ranks each threat by a stated exploit path and caps path-less threats at medium as hypotheses.
Does not delete or suppress low-ranked threats; records them with what would confirm a higher rank.
